trigger: Writing a new lesson via assistant-curator.py
rule: DO NOT pass --pin by default. Pin only when (a) Mukul explicitly says 'remember this' / 'pin' / 'always' / 'never' or names the rule out loud, or (b) the rule is a security guardrail (refusing destructive ops, classifier bypass, allowlist widening) where decay-via-archive would be unsafe. Everything else gets unpinned by default and the Curator's stale-sweep can archive it after 30 days of disuse. Pinning everything defeats the trim mechanism — Mukul flagged 32 lessons as too many, and 22 of those got cut because they were just transient design observations, not durable rules.
why: On 2026-05-22 Mukul: 'you should really trim your lessons, we are on #35'. Diagnosis: I'd been pinning every lesson out of habit, which made the Curator's 30-day stale-sweep guard refuse to archive any of them. Result: lesson list was a build journal instead of a rules file. Trimmed 32→10 by unpinning + archiving anything whose rule was already encoded in production code (Triage prompt / settings.json / Operating Guide / world-scanner). Default pin=False prevents recurrence.
